titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Franklin Henry Giddings Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday December 9, 2019 at 06:06

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Bradford H. Gray on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Franklin Henry Giddings Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Franklin Henry Giddings Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

bioghist.head: Franklin Henry Giddings
bioghist.p: Franklin Henry Giddings (1855-1931) was an American sociologist and economist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of personal papers, personal correspondence, biographical and family papers, photographs, professional correspondence, writings, offprints, ephemera, and other printed materials documenting the life, career, and family of American economist and sociologist Franklin Henry Giddings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Personal material and correspondence, 1836-1931. II. Biographical and family material, 1890s-1937. III. Correspondence and papers relating to public affairs, 1870s-1968. IV. Professional correspondence, 1842-1994. V. Writings, 1870-1936. VI. Offprints, 1878-1936.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence with family and friends, including Margaret Giddings, condolence letters to Margaret Giddings following Franklin Henry Giddings' death, and other personal papers and correspondence.

did.unittitle: Pocket-sized New Testament (New York: American Bible Society, 1865) and Holy Bible (Cincinnati: E. Morgan and Co., 1838)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pocket-sized New Testament is missing cover. Holy Bible contains marginalia on book boards, as well as an inserted note on pink paper.

did.unittitle: The National Reader; A Selection of Excercises in Reading and Speaking (Boston: Charles Bowen Publisher, 1836)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The National Reader is missing both covers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains approximately 30 letters from various individuals.

did.unittitle: Personal letters to and from Franklin Henry Giddings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from Franklin H. Giddings' mother and family, as well as Barnard students.

did.unittitle: Personal letters to and from Franklin Henry Giddings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Mostly contains correspondence from Franklin H. Giddings to Margaret Giddings. Also contains correspondence from J. Williams, of Hobart College, to Franklin H. Giddings.

did.unittitle: Gems For The Bridal Ring , compiled by Rev. J.E. Rankin (Boston: Congregational Sabbath School and Publishing Society, 1868)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a pasted-in mariage certificate, between Franklin H. Giddings and Lizzie P. Hawes, dated November 8, 1876.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various family correspondence, including discriptions the 1896 visit to Herbert Spencer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes tintype photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Regarding Franklin H. Gidding's death.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various correspondence, as well as a pocket-sized New Testament , (New York: James Pott and Co.), autographed by Franklin H. Giddings. Also contains: Franklin H. Giddings' 1870 journal, which has a placed in carte de visite of Ada Torrey, the first cousin of Rebecca Jane Fuller Giddings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various correspondence. Also contains carte de visites and photographs.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains family correspondence, genealogical materials, Giddings biographical materials, clippings, and other papers related to Giddings and the Giddings family.

did.unittitle: Dad - 1931: C and A, The War and After

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies of The New York Genealogical and Biographical Record , Vol. XLII, No. 4 (October 1911). Also contains Columbia University: In the City of New York, report of the Dean of Barnard College (New York: Morningside Heights, 1937) and clippings concerning Franklin H. Giddings.

did.unittitle: "Re: F.H.G. (Book) - C and A - F.H.G. (Lake to N.Y., July '33)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, various writings, clippings, and printed material.

did.unittitle: "F.H.G. - autobiographical sketch and other materials used in Chaps I and II"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings, photographs, writings and various printed materials.

did.unittitle: Local Health Work - India: State of Travancore

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, various writings, clippings, and printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Franklin H. Giddings' wallet, as well as various writings and printed materials.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the convocation program of Columbia University for October 31, 1929. Also contains, various correspondence, including correspondence from Franklin H. Gidding's students, and clippings regarding Franklin H. Giddings.

did.unittitle: "Take to N.Y. (Book) - Odds and Ends re: F.H.G."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, various writings, clippings, and printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, photographs, various writings, clippings, and printed material.

did.unittitle: Correspondence and papers relating to public affairs

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence and other papers related to Gidding's work in public policy on a variety of topics. Also includes a file of "crackpot" letters.

did.unittitle: F.H.G. Library - gift to the University of North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a list of books sent to the University of North Carolina. It is an inventory of a substantial part of Franklin H. Gidding' professional library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various correspondence and professional papers.

did.unittitle: Responses to F.H.G. books and articles

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains 1925 correspondence from colleagues concerning Franklin H. Giddings' 70th birthday. Included is a John Bates Clark letter.

did.unittitle: F.H.G. - miscellaneous, notes and drafts on Economics and other notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various correspondence.

did.unittitle: Culver lectures (Brown 1917) and publication of The Responsible State
Sociology in China, Japan and India

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence and a photograph.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to Franklin H. Giddings and Margaret Giddings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains clippings, some manuscripts and various printed material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a letter from Kennosuke Sato, a Japanese writer and overseas journalist who attended Columbia University in 1921.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains Giddings' professional correspondence. Correspondents include Jane Addams,Theodore Roosevelt, William H. Taft, Cornelius Vanderbilt, Jr., and Gregory Zilboorg, among many others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a 4 May 1994 letter from Robert H. Rubin Books to Brad Gray, regarding the possible sale of material. Also included are photocopies of some of that material. Specifically, Simon Patten and Böhm Bawerk letters, and a Supplementary of Franklin H. Giddings' manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains various correspondence, including letters pertaining to James Mark Baldwin's dictionary and a dispute with Franklin H. Gibbings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains a letter from Franklin H. Giddings to the "Membership Committee of the Author's Club", care of Stephen Henry Thayer.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence regarding Hob House.

did.unittitle: Ward - Giddings re Ward (Iva L. Peters Correspondence)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from J. M. Williams, a former strudent of Franklin H. Giddings. Also contains some replies by Franklin H. Giddings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence from sociologists, regarding intellectual matters, academic politics and publications.

did.unittitle: Social scientists (appended list to Part I)
Free Speech - Emma Goldman / Scott Nearing
Birth Control, Eugenics, and Margaret Sanger

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescripts, clippings, notes, lectures, lists, biographical sketches, corrections, and other writings by Giddings and others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the tie of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains the last copies of the New York World newspaper, and various engravings of individuals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains typescript in envelope.

did.unittitle: Correspondence from the League to Enforce Peace
Early notes and drafts on philosophy
Typescripts of 30 Short Articles - including "Tocqueville", immigration, etc.
Review of "The Menace of Privilege" by Henry George, Jr.
Greatest Reformation Period in Human History - draft
"Family That Will Be" - draft, notes on divorce
"The Western Hemisphere In the World of Tomorrow" - typescript
"Cost of Production of Capital" - two uncorrected typescripts
Notes and outlines for book on religion

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains images of Herbert Spencer, Lester Frank Ward, Albion Woodbury Small, and William Graham Sumner.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Materials roughly maintain the order found at the time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains offprints of articles on sociology, education, evolution, economics, public policy, and other topics by Giddings and others.

did.unittitle: Pagan Poems , by Franklin H. Giddings (New York: The Macmillan Company, 1914)
Twentieth Century magazine, Vol. III, No. 6

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains two copies.

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: A Political Program by Franklin H. Giddings (1900), The Psychological Review , Vol. VIII, No. 4 (July 1901), the cover of The Measurement of Social Forces , by Franklin H. Giddings (1 November 1922), and The American Journal of Sociology , Vol. XXXVII, No. 3 (November 1931).

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: The Theory of Socialization by Franklin H. Giddings (1897); Education: The Need of the South by Dexter A. Hawkins (1878); Social Science Association of Philadelphia: Papers of 1874 - Methods of Valuation of RFAL Estate for Taxation by Thomas Cochran (1874); The Course of Biologic Evolution by Lester F. Ward (1890); "Lectures on Economics" by Robert Erskine Ely; "The Changing Attitude Toward War As Reflected in the American Press"; two sample document copies of The Encyclopaedia of Original Documents .

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: Pluralistic Behavior by Franklin H. Giddings (1920); two copies The Educational Value of Sociology by Franklin H. Giddings (1922); The Grounds of Presumption by Franklin H. Giddings (1922); An Intensive Sociology: A Project by Franklin H. Giddings (1930); two copies of Civilization and Society: An Account of the Development and Behavior of the Human Society arranged and edited by Howard W. Odum; Bibliography of Franklin Henry Giddings (1931); ten copies of A Political Program by Franklin H. Giddings (1900); The Growth of Capital and the Cause of Interest by Franklin H. Giddings (1891); Inhabitants and Societies by Franklin H. Giddings (1931); Franklin Henry Giddings: 1851-1931 by Alvan A. Tenney (1931); Lester F. Ward review of "The Theory of the Leisure Class" (1899).

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: A Theory of Social Causation by Franklin H. Giddings (1904); The Relation of Social Democracy to the Higher Education by Franklin H. Giddings (1894); a book of clippings and notes entitled "Scraps: Contributions to Union College Spectator, etc. - F.H.G."

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains: Giddings, Ward, and Small: An Interchange of Letters edited by Bernhard J. Stern (1932); The Letters of Albion W. Small to Lester F. Ward III edited by Bernhard J. Stern (1936); The Course of Biologic Evolution by Lester F. Ward.

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents

did.unittitle: Reprints, clippings, and various printed material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
